## Attention Cornell Video Users

University Relations is no longer accepting submissions to CornellVideo.

Moving forward, we urge you to use the Video On Demand (VOD) service provided by CIT to host your video content. Instructions for uploading to VOD and general information about the platform can be found at the [Video on Demand website](https://it.cornell.edu/vod) .

We are working with CIT to determine the best path toward migrating existing videos in the Cornell Video collection to VOD. Owners of Cornell Video collections will be contacted in the coming weeks to facilitate this effort.

If you have questions or concerns, please contact the Digital Experience team at cornellcast@cornell.edu .

## Harmonizing Food Systems With Human Health Through Industry-University Partnerships

Posted on January 9, 2012 By College of Agriculture and Life Sciences (CALS) , College of Human Ecology , Division of Nutritional Sciences

### We've received your request

You will be notified by email when the transcript and captions are available. The process may take up to 5 business days. Please contact cornellcast@cornell.edu if you have any questions about this request.

An October 28, 2011 symposium at Cornell addressed food systems and their affect on human health.

Keynote speakers included Frank Hu, professor of nutrition and epidemiology at Harvard University, and Dondeena Bradley, vice president of global design and development in Nutrition Ventures for PepsiCo. Cornell professors Christine Olson, Per Pinstrup-Andersen, David Levitsky and Olga Padilla-Zakour were panelists.

The event was hosted by the Department of Food Science and the Division of Nutritional Sciences.

### Related Links

* [Cornell Chronicle: Food systems need better sync with health](http://www.news.cornell.edu/stories/Nov11/FoodSymposium.html)
